record.finding: Higgayon (H1902H) operates as a directionally-determined cognitive act — the same reflective faculty deployed in devout meditation toward God (975-001) and in hostile scheming against others (5883-001). The act is morally neutral in itself; direction determines its character. This is Sub-pattern A of the directionally-determined inner faculty pattern identified across C02.

operations.description: Session B pointer — meditation: higgayon as directionally-determined cognitive act

record.finding: Dialogismos (G1261) displays the full range of its directionally-determined character in C02: 1081-001 names corrupt inner reasoning originating in the heart (Moral/Conscience) and 1081-002 names the mind's legitimate deliberation on contested matters (Cognitive/Mind). Same root, same faculty, opposite moral orientation. Session B should treat dialogismos as a directionally-determined term whose dimension shifts with context.

operations.description: Session B pointer — reasoning: dialogismos corrupt vs. legitimate poles

record.finding: Registry 160 (thought) contains three instances of the directionally-determined inner faculty pattern: (1) re.a (H7454) — 3453-001 names thoughts known by God and God's thoughts precious to the psalmist, the same relational faculty operating from both sides of the divine-human boundary simultaneously (Sub-pattern C); (2) dialogismos (G1261) — 3409-001 names inner thoughts as site of spiritual warfare and accusation (Moral/Conscience) and 3410-001 names inner reckoning as the ground of faith (Cognitive/Mind) — same root, opposite orientation; (3) fronimos (G5429) pattern carries from C01: prudence and craftiness as the same perceptive faculty (Sub-pattern B). Session B must treat these as analytically distinct uses of the same terms.

operations.description: Session B pointer — thought: directionally-determined faculty instances in R160

record.finding: Registry 174 (wisdom) contains multiple directionally-determined term pairs: (1) a.rum (H6175) and or.mah (H6195) — prudence as cognitive faculty vs. craftiness as moral orientation toward deception (Sub-pattern B); (2) sofos/sofia (G4680/G4678) — wisdom toward God (4458-001, Moral/Conscience character type) vs. wisdom turned inward as self-deception (4458-002, Moral/Conscience distortion); (3) cha.kham (H2449/H2450) — wisdom from fear of Lord (528-003, Spiritual/God-ward) vs. wisdom self-attributed (528-003 note). The cluster reveals that wisdom vocabulary in Scripture is structurally shaped by the direction of the soul's orientation. Session B should treat wisdom terms as directionally-sensitive throughout.

operations.description: Session B pointer — wisdom: directionally-determined faculty pattern across R174

record.description: Directionally-determined inner faculty pattern — a recurring structural principle identified across C02 registries (108, 127, 160, 174, 49, 126): the same inner faculty or act appears in morally or directionally opposite forms within a single term. Three sub-patterns: (A) directional opposition — same reflective act, Godward vs. against another; (B) capacity and its corruption — prudence vs. craftiness as the same perceptive faculty; (C) relational symmetry — a term operating from both sides of the divine-human boundary simultaneously. Open question for Session D: is this a new dimension of the inner life, or a structural principle about how inner dimensions work? Cannot be resolved from C02 data alone. Requires cross-cluster synthesis to assess scope and implications.

operations.description: Session D pointer — directionally-determined faculty pattern: cross-cluster synthesis question
